repo_notes.content: Tellson's Bank is a synthetic 12-service banking monorepo. Documentation should emphasize the four compliance-critical path families: transaction processing, authentication, PII handling, and audit logging. The repo intentionally has uneven test maturity, including audit-logger with no pytest, coverage configuration, mocks, or CI job.
repo_notes.author: Tellson's platform team

repo_notes.content: Prioritize docs/compliance/critical-paths.md, docs/architecture/service-catalog.md, docs/architecture/compliance-data-flow.md, docs/compliance/coverage-baseline.md, AGENTS.md, .agents/skills, and GitHub workflows for coverage ratchets, issue triggers, PR QA, change-triggered coverage, release gates, and coverage swarms. Treat generated dependency folders, coverage reports, build outputs, and .local-operator as non-product artifacts.
repo_notes.author: Tellson's platform team

repo_notes.content: The most important narrative is how coverage work moves from repo comprehension to encoded test conventions, service-specific tests, pull-request evidence, and CI ratchet automation. Documentation should call out where Java, TypeScript, and Python services differ in test setup.
repo_notes.author: Tellson's platform team

pages.title: Architecture Overview
pages.purpose: Document the Tellson's Bank monorepo structure, shared models, service ownership, and cross-service dependency graph.

pages.title: Compliance-Critical Paths
pages.purpose: Document transaction processing, authentication, PII handling, and audit logging paths, including the primary and supporting services for each path.

pages.title: Service Families
pages.purpose: Document the Java, TypeScript, and Python services, their test infrastructure, and how each service contributes to a compliance path.

pages.title: Coverage Baseline
pages.purpose: Document baseline coverage posture, targeted branch-coverage gaps, and why repo-level coverage is not enough for compliance-path work.

pages.title: Audit Logger Bootstrap
pages.purpose: Document audit-logger's missing test infrastructure and the expected first remediation work: pytest, coverage, mocks, and CI without production-code changes.

pages.title: CI Coverage Ratchet
pages.purpose: Document the coverage gate, GitHub Actions workflows, Devin API trigger scripts, PR/commit coverage triggers, QA review triggers, coverage swarm dispatch, and how below-threshold paths open remediation sessions.

pages.title: Agent Testing Conventions
pages.purpose: Document AGENTS.md and .agents/skills guidance: synthetic fixtures only, no production-code changes for test PRs, and targeted coverage evidence requirements.
